Unpacking Henads in NeoplatonismI. The shared starting point  II. Iamblichus: gods beyond being, but manyIII. Proclus: henads explicitly named — and still not beingsIV. Proclus on divine individuality without determinationV. So what are henads, if not beings?VI. Why this resolves the Plotinus problem

Proclus Mereology Explained1. The big shift: from intelligibility to ontology2. Contemporary metaphysics of structure3. Contemporary theory of mind4. The real missing piece: formal causality5. Why the ancient problem keeps returning6. The deep differenceFinal summary1. “The true is the whole.”  2. “The whole is nothing other than the essence consummating itself through its development.”  3. “The individual is what he is only through the universal.”  4. “What is rational is actual; and what is actual is rational.”  5. “The real is not arbitrary, but necessary.”  6. “To comprehend what is, this is the task of philosophy, for what is, is reason.”  7. “The understanding determines and holds the determinations fixed; reason is negative and dialectical.”  8. “The abstract understanding counts, but it does not comprehend.”  9. “To know a thing does not mean merely to be acquainted with it, but to grasp its necessity.”  10. “Substance is essentially subject.”  11. “Spirit is the self-knowing, actual Idea.”  12. “The Absolute is not a substance but a subject.”  The deep continuity
